get.summary: Inspect the authenticated caller's claims.
get.description: Returns the email, tenant_id and subject extracted from the bearer JWT — useful for verifying token generation and validation.

200.description: Success

post.summary: Request a pre-signed S3 upload URL
post.description: This endpoint issues a presigned S3 upload URL (per ADR-004 — client-direct upload, no Lambda proxy). The caller receives a short-lived URL and must PUT the binary directly to S3. The upload is not confirmed until ConfirmAssetUpload is called (or SQS triggers it automatically). Document the URL TTL behaviour if known.

202.description: The request was accepted and a pre-signed upload URL was issued.

400.description: The request is invalid, for example required fields are missing or malformed.

403.description: The caller is authenticated but does not have permission to request a pre-signed upload URL.

post.description: Are tags additive (new tags appended to existing set) or does this replace all tags? Document the distinction between additive and replace semantics so consumers know whether to include existing tags when submitting.

parameters.name: assetId
parameters.description: The unique identifier of the asset to tag.

202.description: The tag request was accepted and the asset will be updated with the new tags shortly.

400.description: The request is invalid, for example required fields are missing or malformed.

403.description: The caller is authenticated but does not have permission to tag the asset.

404.description: The specified asset was not found.

post.summary: Initiate a multipart S3 upload for a large asset
post.description: Starts an S3 multipart upload session for assets too large for a single PUT (typically > 100 MB). Returns pre-signed URLs for each part. The client uploads parts concurrently, then calls POST /assets/{id}/multipart/complete with the ETags returned by S3. Part size is server-configured (default 50 MB). Part URLs expire after 15 minutes.

202.description: Multipart session created. Upload parts using the returned URLs.

400.description: Invalid request — bad fileName, unsupported contentType, or size exceeds profile limit.

403.description: Caller does not have permission.

404.description: mediaItemId not found.

409.description: MediaItem is archived.

post.summary: Detach an asset from a media item.
post.description: removes the asset from its assigned role on the media item. If the asset is not currently attached, 404 is returned (not a 409 — the resource simply does not exist in that relationship). The asset itself is not deleted.

parameters.name: assetId
parameters.description: The unique identifier of the asset to detach.

202.description: The detach request was accepted and the asset is no longer associated with the media item.

400.description: The request is invalid, for example required fields are missing or malformed.

403.description: The caller is authenticated but does not have permission to detach the asset.

404.description: The asset is not currently attached to any media item or does not exist.

delete.description: Deletion removes the asset record and triggers deletion of the S3 object. This cascades to any associated renditions. If the asset is currently attached to a media item, clarify whether detachment is required first or whether delete force-detaches.

parameters.name: assetId
parameters.description: The unique identifier of the asset to delete.

400.description: The request is invalid, for example required fields are missing or malformed.

403.description: The caller is authenticated but does not have permission to delete the asset.

404.description: The specified asset was not found.

get.summary: Get asset details by Id.
get.description: Retrieves detailed information about a specific asset. Returns 404 if the asset does not exist or is not accessible to the caller.

parameters.name: assetId
parameters.description: The unique identifier of the asset to retrieve.

200.description: The asset details were successfully retrieved.

403.description: The caller is authenticated but does not have permission to access the asset.

404.description: No asset with the specified Id exists or is accessible to the caller.

post.description: this endpoint transitions an asset from Pending to Active state. It can be called manually by the client after S3 upload completes, or triggered automatically via SQS when the S3 PutObject event fires. Clarify idempotency behaviour: calling confirm on an already-Active asset returns 409.

parameters.name: assetId
parameters.description: The unique identifier of the asset to confirm.

202.description: The upload confirmation was accepted and the asset state transition has started.

400.description: The request is invalid, for example required fields are missing or malformed.

403.description: The caller is authenticated but does not have permission to confirm the asset upload.

404.description: The specified asset was not found.

409.description: The asset cannot be confirmed because its current state does not allow this transition (e.g. already Active).

post.description: Called after all S3 parts have been uploaded. Provide the ETag returned by S3 for each part. The server calls S3.CompleteMultipartUpload to assemble the final object, then transitions the asset to Validating (virus scan and format validation begin).

parameters.name: assetId
parameters.description: The asset Id returned by the initiate endpoint.

202.description: Upload completed. Asset is now Validating.

400.description: Missing or malformed parts list.

404.description: Asset not found.

409.description: Asset is not in Pending state or is not a multipart upload.

422.description: S3 rejected the completion request (ETag mismatch or missing parts).

403.description: Forbidden

post.summary: Attach an asset to a media item.
post.description: Attaches an asset to a media item.

parameters.name: assetId
parameters.description: Attaching assigns the asset to a named role on the media item. If the role is already occupied by another asset, 409 is returned. Explain role assignment semantics: whether this replaces the existing assignment or requires explicit detach first.

parameters.name: mediaItemId
parameters.description: Gets or sets the media item Id.

202.description: The attach request was accepted and the asset has been attached to the media item.

400.description: The request is invalid, for example required fields are missing or malformed.

403.description: The caller is authenticated but does not have permission to attach the asset to the media item.

409.description: The asset cannot be attached to the media item because its current state does not allow this transition, or because the specified role on the media item is already occupied by another asset.

422.description: A domain-level validation or business rule error occurred.

post.description: Archiving marks the asset as unavailable for new assignments; it does not delete the S3 object. Already-archived assets return 409. Note idempotency expectations

parameters.name: assetId
parameters.description: The unique identifier of the asset to archive.

202.description: The archive request was accepted and the asset state transition has started.

400.description: The request is invalid, for example required fields are missing or malformed.

403.description: The caller is authenticated but does not have permission to archive the asset.

409.description: The asset cannot be archived because its current state does not allow this transition.

422.description: A domain-level validation or business rule error occurred.

post.description: Cancels a pending multipart upload. Releases all S3 part storage and permanently marks the asset as MultipartAborted. This operation is irreversible — the asset cannot be recovered after abort.

parameters.name: assetId
parameters.description: The asset Id returned by the initiate endpoint.

202.description: Multipart upload aborted. Asset is MultipartAborted.

404.description: Asset not found.

409.description: Asset is not in Pending state or is not a multipart upload.

403.description: Forbidden

get.summary: List assets for a media item.
get.description: Retrieves a paginated list of assets associated with the specified media item. Returns an empty list if no assets are found or if the caller does not have access to any assets for the media item.

200.description: A paginated list of asset summaries.

403.description: The caller is authenticated but does not have permission to access any assets for the specified media item.

get.summary: List versions of a media profile.
get.description: Retrieves a paginated list of published version snapshots for a specific media profile. Returns 404 if the media profile does not exist or is not accessible to the caller.

parameters.name: mediaProfileId
parameters.description: The unique identifier of the media profile to list versions for.

200.description: A paginated list of media profile versions.

403.description: The caller is authenticated but does not have permission to access the media profile.

404.description: No media profile with the specified Id exists or is accessible to the caller.

get.summary: List all media profiles.
get.description: Retrieves a paginated list of all media profiles in the tenant, ordered by name. Returns an empty list if no media profiles are found or if the caller does not have access to any media profiles.

200.description: A paginated list of media profile summaries.

403.description: The caller is authenticated but does not have permission to access any media profiles.

post.summary: Created with the profile Id response.
post.description: Document that this initiates the draft lifecycle — a profile must be published before it can be assigned to assets.

400.description: The request is invalid, for example required fields are missing or malformed.

403.description: The caller is authenticated but does not have permission to create a media profile.

409.description: Duplicate profile name within owner namespace.

get.summary: Get a specific version of a media profile.
get.description: Retrieves the details of a specific version of a media profile. Returns 404 if the media profile or the specified version does not exist or is not accessible to the caller.

parameters.name: mediaProfileId
parameters.description: The unique identifier of the media profile.

parameters.name: version
parameters.description: The version number of the media profile to retrieve.

200.description: The details of the specified media profile version were successfully retrieved.

403.description: The caller is authenticated but does not have permission to access the specified media profile version.

404.description: No media profile with the specified Id exists or is accessible to the caller, or no version with the specified number exists for the media profile.

get.summary: Get media profile details by Id.
get.description: Retrieves detailed information about a specific media profile. Returns 404 if the media profile does not exist or is not accessible to the caller.

parameters.name: mediaProfileId
parameters.description: The unique identifier of the media profile to retrieve.

200.description: The media profile details were successfully retrieved.

403.description: The caller is authenticated but does not have permission to access the media profile.

404.description: No media profile with the specified Id exists or is accessible to the caller.

get.summary: Search media items by title and description.
get.description: Performs a full-text search for media items based on the provided search term. The search will match against both the title and description of media items. Returns a paginated list of matching media items. If the search term is empty or consists only of whitespace, a 400 Bad Request response will be returned.

parameters.name: q
parameters.description: The search term to match against media item titles and descriptions.

200.description: A paginated list of media items that match the search criteria.

400.description: The search term is missing or invalid.

403.description: The caller is authenticated but does not have permission to perform this search.

get.summary: List media items not assigned to any folder.
get.description: Retrieves a paginated list of media items for the tenant that have not been assigned to any folder.

parameters.name: page
parameters.description: The page number for pagination (default: 1).

parameters.name: pageSize
parameters.description: The number of items per page (default: 20).

200.description: A paginated list of unassigned media item summaries.

403.description: The caller is authenticated but does not have permission to access media items.

get.summary: List versions of a media item.
get.description: Retrieves a paginated list of versions for a specific media item. Returns 404 if the media item does not exist or is not accessible to the caller.

parameters.name: mediaItemId
parameters.description: The unique identifier of the media item to list versions for.

200.description: A paginated list of media item versions.

403.description: The caller is authenticated but does not have permission to access the media item.

404.description: No media item with the specified Id exists or is accessible to the caller.

get.summary: List media items contained in a specific folder.
get.description: Retrieves a paginated list of media items directly contained within a specific folder for the tenant.

parameters.name: folderId
parameters.description: The unique identifier of the folder to retrieve items from.

parameters.name: page
parameters.description: The page number for pagination (default: 1).

parameters.name: pageSize
parameters.description: The number of items per page (default: 20).

200.description: A paginated list of media item summaries in the specified folder.

403.description: The caller is authenticated but does not have permission to access items in the specified folder.

400.description: Bad Request

post.summary: Create a new media item within a specific folder.
post.description: Creates a media item in Draft state scoped to the specified folder within a collection. The folder must exist and belong to the caller's tenant. Raises MediaItemCreated event.

parameters.name: folderId
parameters.description: The unique identifier of the folder in which to create the media item.

202.description: The media item was successfully created.

400.description: The request is invalid, for example required fields are missing or malformed.

403.description: The caller is authenticated but does not have permission to create a media item in the specified folder.

404.description: The specified folder or media profile was not found.

get.summary: Get details of a specific version of a media item.
get.description: Retrieves detailed information about a specific version of a media item. Returns 404 if the media item or the specified version does not exist or is not accessible to the caller.

parameters.name: mediaItemId
parameters.description: The unique identifier of the media item.

parameters.name: versionNumber
parameters.description: The version number of the media item to retrieve.

200.description: The media item version details were successfully retrieved.

403.description: The caller is authenticated but does not have permission to access the media item version.

404.description: No media item with the specified Id and version number exists or is accessible to the caller.

get.summary: Get media item details by Id.
get.description: Retrieves detailed information about a specific media item. Returns 404 if the media item does not exist or is not accessible to the caller.

parameters.name: mediaItemId
parameters.description: The unique identifier of the media item to retrieve.

200.description: The media item details were successfully retrieved.

403.description: The caller is authenticated but does not have permission to access the media item.

404.description: No media item with the specified Id exists or is accessible to the caller.

get.summary: Get folders within a specific collection or within a specific folder.
get.description: Retrieves a paginated list of folders within a specific collection or within a specific folder.

parameters.name: collectionId
parameters.description: The unique identifier of the collection. If not provided, retrieves folders at the root level.

parameters.name: folderId
parameters.description: The unique identifier of the parent folder. Required if collectionId is provided.

parameters.name: page
parameters.description: The page number for pagination (default: 1).

parameters.name: pageSize
parameters.description: The number of items per page (default: 20).

200.description: A paginated list of folders with type information.

403.description: The caller is authenticated but does not have permission to access items in the specified folder.

400.description: Bad Request

get.summary: Get child folders and media items within a specific folder.
get.description: Retrieves a paginated list of active child folders and media items directly contained within a specific folder. Results include ChildType field ('Folder' or 'MediaItem') to distinguish item types.

parameters.name: folderId
parameters.description: The unique identifier of the parent folder.

parameters.name: page
parameters.description: The page number for pagination (default: 1).

parameters.name: pageSize
parameters.description: The number of items per page (default: 20).

200.description: A paginated list of child folders and media items with type information.

403.description: The caller is authenticated but does not have permission to access items in the specified folder.

400.description: Bad Request

get.summary: Get folder details by Id.
get.description: Retrieves detailed information about a specific folder. Returns 404 if the folder does not exist or is not accessible to the caller.

parameters.name: folderId
parameters.description: The unique identifier of the folder to retrieve.

200.description: The folder details were successfully retrieved.

403.description: The caller is authenticated but does not have permission to access the folder.

404.description: No folder with the specified Id exists or is accessible to the caller.

patch.description: Renames the folder. The new name must be unique within its parent scope. Raises FolderRenamed event.

parameters.name: folderId
parameters.description: The unique identifier of the folder to rename.

400.description: The request is invalid, for example required fields are missing or malformed.

403.description: The caller is authenticated but does not have permission to rename the folder.

404.description: The specified folder was not found.

get.summary: List public collections.
get.description: Retrieves a paginated list of public collections. Returns an empty list if no public collections are found.

200.description: A paginated list of public collection summaries.

403.description: The caller is authenticated but does not have permission to access any public collections.

get.summary: List collections.
get.description: Retrieves a paginated list of collections for the tenant.

parameters.name: page
parameters.description: The page number for pagination.

parameters.name: pageSize
parameters.description: The number of items per page.

200.description: A paginated list of collection summaries matching the specified criteria.

400.description: Invalid query parameters.

403.description: The caller is authenticated but does not have permission to access any collections matching the specified criteria.

post.description: Creates a new top-level collection within the caller's tenant. Collections are the root organisational container for folders and media items. Raises CollectionCreated event.

202.description: Collection created successfully.

400.description: The request is invalid, for example required fields are missing or malformed.

403.description: The caller is authenticated but does not have permission to create a collection.

404.description: Not found.

get.summary: Get collection details by Id.
get.description: Retrieves detailed information about a specific collection. Returns 404 if the collection does not exist or is not accessible to the caller.

parameters.name: collectionId
parameters.description: The unique identifier of the collection to retrieve.

200.description: The collection details were successfully retrieved.

403.description: The caller is authenticated but does not have permission to access the collection.

404.description: No collection with the specified Id exists or is accessible to the caller.

patch.description: Updates mutable properties of a collection (e.g. name, description). Partial update semantics — only fields included in the request body are changed. Raises CollectionUpdated event.

parameters.name: collectionId
parameters.description: The unique identifier of the collection to update.

400.description: The request is invalid, for example required fields are missing or malformed.

403.description: The caller is authenticated but does not have permission to update the collection.

404.description: The specified collection was not found.

put.summary: Update the pinned version of a record type on a media profile.
put.description: Document that pinning controls which published version of the record type schema is enforced for assets using this profile; omitting the pin tracks the latest published version automatically.

parameters.name: mediaProfileId
parameters.description: The unique identifier of the media profile to update a pinned record type version on.

parameters.name: recordTypeId
parameters.description: The unique identifier of the record type to update the pinned version of.

204.description: Pinned record type version updated successfully.

400.description: The request is invalid, for example required fields are missing or malformed.

403.description: The caller is authenticated but does not have permission to update the pinned version of a record type on the specified media profile.

404.description: Record type not attached or version does not exist.

409.description: No active draft

422.description: Pinning to an older version would cause schema incompatibility with existing asset records.

patch.summary: Update an asset definition on a media profile.
patch.description: Clarify that updates to an asset definition on a draft may not narrow constraints that existing assets already violate.

parameters.name: mediaProfileId
parameters.description: The unique identifier of the media profile to update an asset definition on.

parameters.name: roleName
parameters.description: The role name of the asset definition to update.

202.description: Asset definition updated successfully.

400.description: The request is invalid, for example required fields are missing or malformed.

403.description: The caller is authenticated but does not have permission to update an asset definition on the specified media profile.

404.description: The specified media profile or asset definition was not found.

409.description: No active draft.

422.description: Update introduces a breaking change incompatible with existing assets

delete.summary: Remove an asset definition from a media profile.
delete.description: Document that removing an asset definition from the active draft takes effect on next publish. Document that removal is not allowed if it would leave existing assets without a definition.

parameters.name: mediaProfileId
parameters.description: The unique identifier of the media profile to remove an asset definition from.

parameters.name: roleName
parameters.description: The role name of the asset definition to remove.

202.description: Asset definition removed successfully.

400.description: The request is invalid, for example required fields are missing or malformed.

403.description: The caller is authenticated but does not have permission to remove an asset definition from the specified media profile.

404.description: The specified media profile or asset definition was not found.

409.description: Definition is currently set as the profile default — must reassign default first; or no active draft.

422.description: Existing assets have files stored under this definition key.

put.summary: Set the review policy of a media profile.
put.description: Document how the review policy governs the In Review → Approved/Rejected state transitions for media items assigned this profile. Changes apply to draft only.

parameters.name: mediaProfileId
parameters.description: The unique identifier of the media profile to set the review policy for.

202.description: Review policy set successfully.

400.description: The request is invalid, for example required fields are missing or malformed.

403.description: The caller is authenticated but does not have permission to set the review policy for the specified media profile.

404.description: The specified media profile was not found.

409.description: No active draft — review policy can only be changed on a draft.

422.description: Invalid policy — e.g., required reviewer count exceeds configured reviewer pool

put.summary: Set the checkout policy of a media profile.
put.description: Document the checkout policy options (e.g., single-user exclusive lock, multi-user concurrent) and their implications for the asset edit workflow. Changes apply to draft only.

parameters.name: mediaProfileId
parameters.description: The unique identifier of the media profile to set the checkout policy for.

202.description: Checkout policy set successfully.

400.description: The request is invalid, for example required fields are missing or malformed.

403.description: The caller is authenticated but does not have permission to set the checkout policy for the specified media profile.

404.description: The specified media profile was not found.

409.description: No active draft — checkout policy can only be changed on a draft.

422.description: Invalid policy configuration

put.summary: Set the capabilities of a media profile.
put.description: Document the available capability values and that this replaces the full capability set — partial updates are not supported. Changes apply to the draft only and take effect on next publish.

parameters.name: mediaProfileId
parameters.description: The unique identifier of the media profile to set the capabilities for.

202.description: Capabilities set successfully.

400.description: The request is invalid, for example required fields are missing or malformed.

403.description: The caller is authenticated but does not have permission to set capabilities for the specified media profile.

404.description: The specified media profile was not found.

409.description: No active draft — capabilities can only be changed on a draft.

422.description: Capability combination invalid.

put.summary: Set the default asset for an asset definition on a media profile.
put.description: Document that setting a default asset takes effect immediately for assets without an assigned file and that it does not change the assigned file for assets that already have one.

parameters.name: mediaProfileId
parameters.description: The unique identifier of the media profile to set the default asset on.

parameters.name: roleName
parameters.description: The role name of the asset definition to set the default asset on.

202.description: Default asset set successfully.

400.description: The request is invalid, for example required fields are missing or malformed.

403.description: The caller is authenticated but does not have permission to set the default asset for an asset definition on the specified media profile.

404.description: profile not found or definition key not present on draft.

409.description: No active draft.

422.description: Document that the default definition is used when callers don't specify a definition key on upload, and that exactly one definition may be marked as default at a time.

post.summary: Reorder asset definitions on a media profile.
post.description: Document that ordering controls display/precedence in UI consumers.

parameters.name: mediaProfileId
parameters.description: The unique identifier of the media profile to reorder asset definitions on.

400.description: The request is invalid, for example required fields are missing or malformed.

403.description: The caller is authenticated but does not have permission to reorder asset definitions on the specified media profile.

404.description: The specified media profile or one or more asset definitions were not found.

409.description: No active draft.

422.description: Provided ordering does not include all definition keys currently on the draft — must be a complete, exact reordering

post.description: Document that publishing promotes the current draft to the active published version and increments the version number. The previously published version is retained in event history for read-model rebuild.

parameters.name: mediaProfileId
parameters.description: The unique identifier of the media profile to publish.

202.description: Media profile published successfully.

400.description: The request is invalid, for example if the media profile is not in a valid state to be published.

403.description: The caller is authenticated but does not have permission to publish the specified media profile.

404.description: The specified media profile was not found.

409.description: No draft exists to publish.

422.description: Draft fails validation — e.g., missing required asset definitions.

delete.description: Document that discarding a draft is non-destructive to the published version — the profile reverts to its last published state and remains active.

parameters.name: mediaProfileId
parameters.description: The unique identifier of the media profile to discard the draft for.

202.description: Media profile draft discarded successfully.

400.description: The request is invalid, for example if the media profile Id is malformed.

403.description: The caller is authenticated but does not have permission to discard the media profile draft.

404.description: The specified media profile was not found.

409.description: No draft currently exists for this profile.

post.summary: Creates a new draft revision of the specified media profile.
post.description: Document the draft/publish cycle and that the revision inherits the last published version's configuration as its starting state.

parameters.name: mediaProfileId
parameters.description: The unique identifier of the media profile to create a revision for.

202.description: Media profile revision created successfully.

400.description: The request is invalid, for example required fields are missing or malformed.

403.description: The caller is authenticated but does not have permission to create a media profile revision.

404.description: The specified media profile was not found.

409.description: Draft already exists — only one draft is permitted per profile at a time.

422.description: Profile is deprecated and cannot be revised.

delete.summary: Detach a record type from a media profile.
delete.description: Document that detachment prevents new records of this type but does not delete existing records on assets already using this profile.

parameters.name: mediaProfileId
parameters.description: The unique identifier of the media profile to detach a record type from.

parameters.name: recordTypeId
parameters.description: The unique identifier of the record type to detach.

400.description: The request is invalid, for example required fields are missing or malformed.

403.description: The caller is authenticated but does not have permission to detach a record type from the specified media profile.

404.description: The record type not attached to this profile.

409.description: record type not currently attached to this profile draft; or no active draft

422.description: Existing assets under this profile carry records of this type — detach would orphan data

post.summary: Attach a record type to a media profile.
post.description: Document that attaching a record type allows assets using this profile to carry records of that type. Takes effect on publish.

parameters.name: mediaProfileId
parameters.description: The unique identifier of the media profile to attach a record type to.

parameters.name: recordTypeId
parameters.description: The unique identifier of the record type to attach.

400.description: The request is invalid, for example required fields are missing or malformed.

403.description: The caller is authenticated but does not have permission to attach a record type to the specified media profile.

404.description: The specified media profile or record type was not found.

409.description: record type already attached to this profile draft; or no active draft

422.description: Record type is deprecated.

post.description: Document that deprecation prevents new assets from being assigned this profile but does not affect existing assignments.

parameters.name: mediaProfileId
parameters.description: The unique identifier of the media profile to deprecate.

202.description: Media profile deprecated successfully.

400.description: The request is invalid, for example if the media profile is already deprecated.

403.description: The caller is authenticated but does not have permission to deprecate the specified media profile.

404.description: The specified media profile was not found.

409.description: The profile already deprecated or has an active draft — must publish or discard draft first.

422.description: The profile is currently assigned to active assets and cannot be deprecated without reassignment.

post.summary: Add an asset definition to a media profile.
post.description: Document that definitions are added to the active draft and become effective on next publish. Document the definitionKey path param format.

parameters.name: mediaProfileId
parameters.description: The unique identifier of the media profile to add an asset definition to.

202.description: Asset definition added successfully.

400.description: The request is invalid, for example required fields are missing or malformed.

403.description: The caller is authenticated but does not have permission to add an asset definition to the specified media profile.

404.description: The specified media profile was not found.

409.description: Asset definition with this key already exists on the profile draft.

422.description: Invalid definition config — e.g., unsupported media type, missing required constraints

post.description: Withdraws a media item from its current review process, cancelling the associated ChangeRequest and returning the item to Draft state. Only valid when the item is In Review. 409 if the item is not currently under review. Raises MediaItemWithdrawn event.

parameters.name: mediaItemId
parameters.description: The unique identifier of the media item to withdraw.

204.description: The media item was successfully withdrawn.

403.description: The caller is authenticated but does not have permission to withdraw the media item.

404.description: No media item exists with the specified Id.

409.description: The media item cannot be withdrawn because its current state does not allow this transition.

patch.summary: Update the title of a media item.
patch.description: Updates the display title of the media item. No state restriction — title can be updated regardless of lifecycle state. Raises MediaItemTitleUpdated event.

parameters.name: mediaItemId
parameters.description: The unique identifier of the media item to update.

400.description: The request is invalid, for example required fields are missing or malformed.

403.description: The caller is authenticated but does not have permission to update the media item.

404.description: No media item exists with the specified Id.

delete.summary: Unassign an asset from a media item role.
delete.description: Removes an asset from its assigned role on the media item. 404 if the specified asset is not currently assigned to the specified role. The asset itself is not deleted.

parameters.name: mediaItemId
parameters.description: The unique identifier of the media item.

parameters.name: roleName
parameters.description: The name of the role to unassign the asset from.

parameters.name: assetId
parameters.description: The unique identifier of the asset to unassign.

204.description: The asset was successfully unassigned from the media item role.

400.description: The request is invalid, for example required fields are missing or malformed.

403.description: The caller is authenticated but does not have permission to unassign the asset from the media item role.

404.description: The specified media item, role, or asset does not exist, or the asset is not assigned to the role.

post.summary: Add or update tags on a media item.
post.description: Applies tags to the media item. Document whether semantics are additive (new tags merged with existing) or replace (existing tags overwritten). Raises MediaItemTagged event.

parameters.name: mediaItemId
parameters.description: The unique identifier of the media item to tag.

204.description: The tags were successfully added or updated on the media item.

400.description: The request is invalid, for example required fields are missing or malformed.

403.description: The caller is authenticated but does not have permission to tag the media item.

404.description: The specified media item was not found.

post.summary: Submit a media item for review.
post.description: Submits the media item for formal review, creating a ChangeRequest. The item must be in Draft or Revised state and not currently checked out. Creates a ChangeRequest with the configured review policy applied (reviewer assignment, approval threshold). Raises MediaItemSubmittedForReview event.

parameters.name: mediaItemId
parameters.description: The unique identifier of the media item to submit for review.

204.description: The submit for review request was accepted and the media item state transition has started.

400.description: The request is invalid, for example required fields are missing or malformed.

403.description: The caller is authenticated but does not have permission to submit the media item for review.

404.description: The specified media item was not found.

409.description: The media item cannot be submitted for review because its current state does not allow this transition.

patch.summary: Set a metadata field on a media item.
patch.description: Sets the value for a single metadata field on the media item. The field must be defined in the Record Type schema attached to the media item's profile. 422 is returned when the value fails schema validation (type mismatch, constraint violation). 404 when the field name is not defined in the schema.

parameters.name: mediaItemId
parameters.description: The unique identifier of the media item.

parameters.name: fieldName
parameters.description: The name of the metadata field to set.

204.description: The metadata field was successfully set.

400.description: The request is invalid, for example required fields are missing or malformed.

403.description: The caller is authenticated but does not have permission to set the metadata field.

404.description: The specified media item was not found.

422.description: The specified metadata field value is invalid.

put.summary: Set multiple metadata fields for a media item.
put.description: Replaces the entire metadata payload for the media item in a single operation. All fields in the request are validated against the Record Type schema. If any field fails validation, the entire batch is rejected (422) and no changes are persisted. Prefer this over multiple SetMetadataField calls when updating several fields atomically.

parameters.name: mediaItemId
parameters.description: The unique identifier of the media item to update.

400.description: The request is invalid, for example required fields are missing or malformed.

403.description: The caller is authenticated but does not have permission to update the media item.

404.description: A media item with the specified Id does not exist.

422.description: A domain-level validation or business rule error occurred.

post.summary: Request check-in of a media item currently checked out by another member.
post.description: A non-checkout-holder user requests that the current checkout holder check in. This notifies the holder without forcibly releasing the lock. Used when another user needs access urgently. 409 if the item is not checked out, or if the requester is the current holder (they should use CheckIn instead).

parameters.name: mediaItemId
parameters.description: The unique identifier of the media item for which to request check-in.

204.description: The request was successful and the check-in request has been created.

403.description: The caller is authenticated but does not have permission to request check-in for the media item.

404.description: The specified media item does not exist.

409.description: The media item is not currently checked out or the current checkout does not allow check-in requests.

post.description: Records a rejection decision against the media item's active ChangeRequest. Rejection reason is required (400 if absent). Returns the item to Draft state for revision. 409 if the item is not In Review. Raises MediaItemRejected event.

parameters.name: mediaItemId
parameters.description: The unique identifier of the media item to reject.

202.description: The reject request was accepted and the media item state transition has started.

400.description: The request is invalid, for example required fields are missing or malformed.

403.description: The caller is authenticated but does not have permission to reject the media item.

404.description: A media item with the specified Id does not exist.

409.description: The media item cannot be rejected because its current state does not allow this transition.

post.summary: Force release checkout on a media item.
post.description: Administratively releases a checkout lock held by any user. Requires elevated permissions (document the required permission/role). 409 if the item is not currently checked out. This is the escalation path after RequestCheckIn is ignored. Raises CheckoutForceReleased event.

parameters.name: mediaItemId
parameters.description: The unique identifier of the media item to force release checkout on.

204.description: The force release checkout request was successful and the media item is now available.

403.description: The caller is authenticated but does not have permission to force release checkout on the media item.

404.description: The specified media item was not found.

409.description: The media item cannot be force released because its current state does not allow this transition.

post.description: Explains item is created in Draft state within the caller's tenant, requires a valid MediaProfileId, raises MediaItemCreated event.

202.description: The media item was successfully created.

400.description: The request is invalid, for example required fields are missing or malformed.

403.description: The caller is authenticated but does not have permission to create a media item.

404.description: The specified folder or media profile was not found.

post.description: Acquires an exclusive checkout lock on the media item. Only one user can hold a checkout at a time. Returns 409 if already checked out — regardless of who holds the checkout. The checkout can be released via CheckIn, AbandonCheckout, or ForceRelease. Raises MediaItemCheckedOut event.

parameters.name: mediaItemId
parameters.description: The unique identifier of the media item to check out.

204.description: The check-out request was successful and the media item is now assigned to the member.

400.description: The request is invalid, for example required fields are missing or malformed.

403.description: The caller is authenticated but does not have permission to check out the media item.

404.description: The specified media item does not exist.

409.description: The media item cannot be checked out because its current state does not allow this transition (e.g., it's already checked out).

post.description: releases the checkout lock held by the calling user, returning the media item to available state. 409 if the item is not currently checked out by the caller (either not checked out at all, or checked out by another user). Use ForceReleaseCheckout to release another user's checkout.

parameters.name: mediaItemId
parameters.description: The unique identifier of the media item to check in.

204.description: The check-in request was successful and the media item is now available.

403.description: The caller is authenticated but does not have permission to check in the media item.

404.description: The specified media item does not exist.

409.description: The media item cannot be checked in because its current state does not allow this transition (e.g., it is not currently checked out).

put.summary: Assign or move a media item to a folder.
put.description: Assigns a media item to a folder, or moves it from its current folder to a new one. If the item is already in the target folder, the operation is a no-op. Target folder must exist within the same collection.

parameters.name: mediaItemId
parameters.description: The unique identifier of the media item to assign or move.

204.description: The media item was successfully assigned or moved to the folder.

400.description: The request is invalid, for example required fields are missing or malformed.

403.description: The caller is authenticated but does not have permission to assign or move the media item.

404.description: The specified media item or folder was not found.

post.summary: Assign an asset to a role for a media item.
post.description: assigns an asset to a named role on the media item as defined in the media profile's asset definitions. Returns 409 if the role already has an assigned asset — use UnassignAssetFromRole first. The role name must match a defined asset definition in the profile.

parameters.name: mediaItemId
parameters.description: The unique identifier of the media item.

parameters.name: roleName
parameters.description: The name of the role to which the asset will be assigned.

204.description: The asset was successfully assigned to the role.

400.description: The request is invalid, for example required fields are missing or malformed.

403.description: The caller is authenticated but does not have permission to assign assets to roles for this media item.

404.description: The specified media item or role was not found.

409.description: The asset cannot be assigned to the role due to a conflict with the current state of the media item or asset.

post.description: Archives the media item, making it read-only and removing it from active workflows. Cannot archive while checked out. Already-archived items return 409. Assets and metadata are retained but the item is no longer modifiable. Raises MediaItemArchived event.

parameters.name: mediaItemId
parameters.description: The unique identifier of the media item to archive.

202.description: The archive request was accepted and the media item state transition has started.

403.description: The caller is authenticated but does not have permission to archive the media item.

404.description: No media item exists with the specified Id.

409.description: The media item cannot be archived because its current state does not allow this transition.

post.description: records an approval decision against the media item's active ChangeRequest. The caller must be an assigned reviewer. Once the configured approval threshold is met, the media item transitions to Approved state. 409 if the item is not In Review. Raises MediaItemApproved event.

parameters.name: mediaItemId
parameters.description: The unique identifier of the media item to approve.

202.description: The approve request was accepted and the media item state transition has started.

403.description: The caller is authenticated but does not have permission to approve the media item.

404.description: A media item with the specified Id does not exist.

409.description: The media item cannot be approved because its current state does not allow this transition.

post.description: The calling user abandons their own checkout, discarding any uncommitted changes and releasing the lock. Unlike CheckIn, abandon signals the work was not completed. 409 if the caller does not hold the current checkout.

parameters.name: mediaItemId
parameters.description: The unique identifier of the media item for which to abandon the checkout.

204.description: The abandon checkout request was accepted and processed successfully.

403.description: The caller is authenticated but does not have permission to abandon the checkout for this media item.

404.description: The specified media item does not exist.

409.description: The checkout cannot be abandoned because its current state does not allow this transition, for example if the media item is not currently checked out or is checked out by another user.

patch.summary: Update the description of a folder.
patch.description: Updates the description text for the folder. An empty string clears the description. Raises FolderDescriptionUpdated event.

parameters.name: folderId
parameters.description: The unique identifier of the folder to update.

204.description: The folder description was successfully updated.

400.description: The request is invalid, for example required fields are missing or malformed.

403.description: The caller is authenticated but does not have permission to update the folder description.

404.description: The specified folder was not found.

put.summary: Move a folder to a new parent folder or collection.
put.description: Moves the folder to a new parent within the same collection. The domain guards against circular references: if the target parent is itself a descendant of the folder being moved, 409 is returned. Moving to the root of the collection is achieved by clearing the parent. Raises FolderMoved event.

parameters.name: folderId
parameters.description: The unique identifier of the folder to move.

400.description: The request is invalid, for example required fields are missing or malformed.

403.description: The caller is authenticated but does not have permission to move the folder.

404.description: The specified folder or collection was not found.

409.description: Circular reference: target parent is a descendant of the folder being moved

post.description: Creates a new folder within the specified collection. Folders provide the hierarchical namespace for organising media items. Can optionally specify a parent folder to create a nested subfolder. Raises FolderCreated event.

parameters.name: collectionId
parameters.description: The unique identifier of the collection to create the folder in.

202.description: Folder created successfully.

400.description: The request is invalid, for example required fields are missing or malformed.

403.description: The caller is authenticated but does not have permission to create a folder in the specified collection or parent folder.

404.description: The specified collection or parent folder was not found.

post.description: Archives the folder and all its contents. Archiving cascades to all child folders and the media items contained within them — document this cascade behaviour explicitly. Already-archived folders return 409. Raises FolderArchived event (and cascade events for child content).

parameters.name: folderId
parameters.description: The unique identifier of the folder to archive.

403.description: The caller is authenticated but does not have permission to archive the folder.

404.description: The specified folder was not found.

409.description: The folder already archived.

post.summary: Add or update tags on a collection.
post.description: Applies tags to the collection. Document additive vs replace semantics (consistent with TagAsset and TagMediaItem). Raises CollectionTagged event.

parameters.name: collectionId
parameters.description: The unique identifier of the collection to tag.

400.description: The request is invalid, for example required fields are missing or malformed.

403.description: The caller is authenticated but does not have permission to tag the collection.

404.description: The specified collection was not found.

put.summary: Set the default media profile for a collection.
put.description: sets the default media profile for new media items created within this collection. The profile must exist and be published. New media items created in the collection without an explicit profile will inherit this default.

parameters.name: collectionId
parameters.description: The unique identifier of the collection.

400.description: The request is invalid, for example required fields are missing or malformed.

403.description: The caller is authenticated but does not have permission to set the default media profile.

404.description: The specified collection or media profile was not found.

post.description: Archives the collection, preventing new media items from being added. Already-archived collections return 409. Raises CollectionArchived event.

parameters.name: collectionId
parameters.description: The unique identifier of the collection to archive.

202.description: Collection archived successfully.

400.description: The request is invalid, for example required fields are missing or malformed.

403.description: The caller is authenticated but does not have permission to archive the collection.

404.description: The specified collection was not found.

409.description: The collection cannot be archived in its current state (e.g. already archived).

get.summary: List comments for a media change request.
get.description: Retrieves a paginated list of comments associated with a specific media change request. Returns 404 if the media change request does not exist or is not accessible to the caller.

parameters.name: changeRequestId
parameters.description: The unique identifier of the media change request.

200.description: A paginated list of comments for the specified media change request.

403.description: The caller is authenticated but does not have permission to access the media change request or its comments.

404.description: No media change request with the specified Id exists or is accessible to the caller.

post.summary: Add a comment to a change request.
post.description: Document that comments are visible to all assigned reviewers and the change request author. Document the commentId in the response body.

parameters.name: changeRequestId
parameters.description: The unique identifier of the change request to which the comment will be added.

400.description: Empty body or exceeds max length.

403.description: The caller is authenticated but does not have permission to add a comment to the change request.

404.description: The specified change request or parent comment (if ParentCommentId is provided) was not found.

422.description: The change request is in a terminal state — comments may not be added after resolution.

get.summary: List media change requests for a media item or owned by the caller.
get.description: Retrieves a paginated list of media change requests. If a mediaItemId is provided, returns change requests associated with the specified media item. If no mediaItemId is provided, returns change requests that are owned by the caller. Returns an empty list if no matching media change requests are found or if the caller does not have access to any matching media change requests.

parameters.name: mediaItemId
parameters.description: The unique identifier of the media item to filter change requests by. If not provided, change requests owned by the caller will be returned.

200.description: A paginated list of media change request summaries.

403.description: The caller is authenticated but does not have permission to access any matching media change requests.

get.summary: Get media change request details by Id.
get.description: Retrieves detailed information about a specific media change request. Returns 404 if the media change request does not exist or is not accessible to the caller.

parameters.name: changeRequestId
parameters.description: The unique identifier of the media change request to retrieve.

200.description: The media change request details were successfully retrieved.

403.description: The caller is authenticated but does not have permission to access the media change request.

404.description: No media change request with the specified Id exists or is accessible to the caller.

post.summary: Withdraw a previously submitted change request review decision.
post.description: Document that withdrawal retracts a previously submitted decision while the change request remains In Review, allowing the reviewer to reassess. Only valid before the overall change request is resolved.

parameters.name: changeRequestId
parameters.description: The unique identifier of the change request for which to withdraw the review.

400.description: The request is invalid, for example required fields are missing or malformed.

403.description: The caller is authenticated but does not have permission to withdraw the review decision.

404.description: The specified change request was not found.

409.description: The reviewer has not yet submitted a review decision on this change request.

422.description: No pending review to withdraw — the change request has already been decided or the caller is not an assigned reviewer

delete.summary: Remove a reviewer from a change request.
delete.description: Document that removing a reviewer before they submit does not affect other assigned reviewers or the overall change request state.

parameters.name: changeRequestId
parameters.description: The unique identifier of the change request.

parameters.name: reviewerId
parameters.description: The unique identifier of the reviewer to remove.

400.description: The request is invalid, for example required fields are missing or malformed.

403.description: The caller is authenticated but does not have permission to remove the reviewer.

404.description: The reviewer not currently assigned to this change request.

422.description: The reviewer has already submitted a review decision — cannot remove after decision is recorded.

post.summary: Assign a reviewer to a change request.
post.description: Document the changeRequestId path param and reviewer identity requirements.

parameters.name: changeRequestId
parameters.description: The unique identifier of the change request.

parameters.name: reviewerId
parameters.description: The unique identifier of the reviewer to assign.

400.description: The request is invalid, for example required fields are missing or malformed.

403.description: The caller is authenticated but does not have permission to assign reviewers to the change request.

404.description: The specified change request or reviewer was not found.

409.description: The reviewer already assigned to this change request.

422.description: The change request is in a terminal state — Approved or Rejected — and can no longer accept reviewer assignments

post.description: Document that a single rejection immediately resolves the change request as Rejected regardless of other pending reviewers, transitioning the linked media item back to Checked Out for correction.

parameters.name: changeRequestId
parameters.description: The unique identifier of the change request for which to reject the review.

400.description: The request is invalid, for example required fields are missing or malformed.

403.description: The caller is authenticated but does not have permission to reject the review.

404.description: The specified change request was not found.

409.description: The reviewer already submitted a decision.

422.description: The not an assigned reviewer; or change request not In Review.

patch.summary: Edit a comment on a change request.
patch.description: Document the commentId path param and that edit history is not retained.

parameters.name: changeRequestId
parameters.description: The unique identifier of the change request containing the comment to edit.

parameters.name: commentId
parameters.description: The unique identifier of the comment to edit.

400.description: Empty body or exceeds max length.

403.description: The caller is not the original comment author.

404.description: The comment not found on this change request.

422.description: The change request is closed — comments are immutable after resolution.

delete.summary: Delete a comment from a change request.
delete.description: Document that deletion is soft — the comment body is cleared but the comment record is retained in the event store for audit purposes.

parameters.name: changeRequestId
parameters.description: The unique identifier of the change request containing the comment to delete.

parameters.name: commentId
parameters.description: The unique identifier of the comment to delete.

400.description: The request is invalid, for example if the IDs are not in the correct format.

403.description: The caller is not the original comment author and does not hold admin role.

404.description: The specified change request or comment was not found.

422.description: The change request is closed — comments are immutable after resolution.

post.description: Document that approval by all assigned reviewers satisfying the review policy triggers automatic state transition of the linked media item to Approved.

parameters.name: changeRequestId
parameters.description: The unique identifier of the change request for which to approve the review.

400.description: The request is invalid, for example required fields are missing or malformed.

403.description: The caller is authenticated but does not have permission to approve the review.

404.description: The specified change request was not found.

409.description: The calling user already submitted a decision on this change request.

422.description: The calling user is not an assigned reviewer on this change request; or change request is no longer In Review.

get.summary: List versions of a record type.
get.description: Retrieves a paginated list of versions for a specific record type. Returns 404 if the record type does not exist or is not accessible to the caller.

parameters.name: recordTypeId
parameters.description: The unique identifier of the record type.

200.description: A paginated list of record type versions.

403.description: The caller is authenticated but does not have permission to access the record type versions.

404.description: No record type with the specified Id exists or is accessible to the caller.

get.summary: List record types owned by the caller.
get.description: Retrieves a paginated list of record types that are owned by the caller. Returns an empty list if no record types are found or if the caller does not have access to any record types.

200.description: A paginated list of record type summaries.

403.description: The caller is authenticated but does not have permission to access any record types.

post.description: Document that a newly created record type begins in Draft state and must be published before it can be attached to a Media Profile. Document the UUID v7 identifier format.

201.description: The record type was successfully created.

400.description: The request is invalid, such as missing required fields or invalid field definitions.

403.description: The caller is authenticated but does not have permission to create record types.

409.description: Duplicate record type name within owner namespace.

get.summary: Get details of a specific version of a record type.
get.description: Retrieves detailed information about a specific version of a record type. Returns 404 if the record type or the specified version does not exist or is not accessible to the caller.

parameters.name: recordTypeId
parameters.description: The unique identifier of the record type.

parameters.name: version
parameters.description: The version number of the record type to retrieve.

200.description: The record type version details were successfully retrieved.

403.description: The caller is authenticated but does not have permission to access the record type version.

404.description: No record type with the specified Id and version exists or is accessible to the caller.

get.summary: Get record type details by Id.
get.description: Retrieves detailed information about a specific record type. Returns 404 if the record type does not exist or is not accessible to the caller.

parameters.name: recordTypeId
parameters.description: The unique identifier of the record type to retrieve.

200.description: The record type details were successfully retrieved.

403.description: The caller is authenticated but does not have permission to access the record type.

404.description: No record type with the specified Id exists or is accessible to the caller.

patch.description: Document that renaming takes effect immediately and does not require a draft/publish cycle. The internal identifier (UUID) is unchanged — existing Media Profile attachments are not affected..

parameters.name: recordTypeId
parameters.description: The unique identifier of the record type to rename.

202.description: The rename request was accepted and the record type rename operation has started.

400.description: The request is malformed or contains invalid data (e.g., missing or invalid new name).

403.description: The caller is authenticated but does not have permission to rename the record type.

404.description: No record type exists with the specified Id.

409.description: New name collides with an existing record type in the owner namespace.

patch.summary: Update a field in a record type.
patch.description: Document the fieldKey path param and that field type changes are not allowed after first publish.

parameters.name: recordTypeId
parameters.description: The unique identifier of the record type containing the field to update.

parameters.name: fieldName
parameters.description: The name of the field to update.

202.description: The field was successfully updated.

400.description: The request is malformed or contains invalid data.

403.description: The caller is authenticated but does not have permission to update the field.

404.description: The field key not found on draft.

409.description: No active draft.

422.description: Update is a breaking change — e.g., narrowing constraints that existing records violate, or changing field type.

delete.summary: Remove a field from a record type.
delete.description: Document that the field is removed from the draft schema; existing record data is not deleted until the record is next updated after the new version publishes.

parameters.name: recordTypeId
parameters.description: The unique identifier of the record type containing the field to remove.

parameters.name: fieldName
parameters.description: The name of the field to remove.

202.description: The field was successfully removed.

400.description: The request is malformed or contains invalid data.

403.description: The caller is authenticated but does not have permission to remove the field.

404.description: The field key not found on draft.

409.description: No active draft.

422.description: The existing asset records populate this field — removing it would cause data loss or schema violation on next validation.

put.summary: Replace a field in a record type.
put.description: Document that replacement is an atomic remove-and-add that preserves field ordering. The old field key is retired and the new key is used for all future records.

parameters.name: recordTypeId
parameters.description: The unique identifier of the record type containing the field to replace.

parameters.name: fieldName
parameters.description: The name of the field to replace.

202.description: The replace request was accepted and the record type update has started.

400.description: The request is malformed or contains invalid data.

403.description: The caller is authenticated but does not have permission to replace the field in the record type.

404.description: Target field key not found on draft.

409.description: New field key already exists; or no active draft.

422.description: The type incompatibility between old and new field — existing record values cannot be coerced.

post.summary: Reorder fields in a record type.
post.description: Document that ordering is cosmetic and affects display order in UI consumers; it does not alter validation or storage behaviour.

parameters.name: recordTypeId
parameters.description: The unique identifier of the record type to update.

202.description: The reorder request was accepted and the record type update has started.

400.description: The request is malformed or contains invalid data.

403.description: The caller is authenticated but does not have permission to reorder fields in the record type.

404.description: No active draft for the specified record type.

409.description: One or more field names do not exist on the active draft.

422.description: Provided ordering is incomplete — must include every field key currently on the draft, including deprecated fields.

delete.summary: Remove a capability from a record type.
delete.description: Document that the change only takes effect on publish.

parameters.name: recordTypeId
parameters.description: The unique identifier of the record type.

parameters.name: capabilityType
parameters.description: The type of capability to remove.

204.description: The capability was successfully removed from the record type.

400.description: The request was malformed or contains invalid parameters.

403.description: The caller is authenticated but does not have permission to remove the capability from the record type.

404.description: The capability not present on the draft.

409.description: No active draft.

422.description: Existing asset records depend on this capability — removal would break validation.

post.description: Document that publishing increments the record type version number. Media Profiles pinned to a specific version are not affected; those tracking latest will automatically see the new version on next enforcement..

parameters.name: recordTypeId
parameters.description: The unique identifier of the record type to publish.

202.description: The record type was successfully published.

400.description: The request is malformed — e.g., the record type Id is not a valid format.

403.description: The caller is authenticated but does not have permission to publish the record type.

404.description: No record type exists with the specified Id.

409.description: No draft exists to publish.

422.description: Draft fails validation — e.g., no fields defined.

delete.description: Document that discarding reverts to the last published version and does not affect any Media Profiles currently attaching this record type.

parameters.name: recordTypeId
parameters.description: The unique identifier of the record type whose draft should be discarded.

202.description: The discard request was accepted and the record type draft discard process has started.

400.description: The request is malformed.

403.description: The caller is authenticated but does not have permission to discard the record type draft.

404.description: No record type exists with the specified Id, or the record type does not have a draft.

409.description: No draft currently exists for this record type.

post.summary: Create a draft of an existing record type.
post.description: Document that the new draft is seeded from the current published version's field and capability configuration.

202.description: The draft was successfully created.

400.description: The request is invalid, such as an invalid record type Id.

403.description: The caller is authenticated but does not have permission to create record type drafts.

404.description: No record type exists with the specified ID.

409.description: A draft already exists for this record type — only one draft permitted at a time.

422.description: The record type is deprecated and cannot be revised.

post.description: Document that deprecation prevents new attachments to Media Profiles and prevents new drafts, but does not remove existing attachments or invalidate existing asset records.

parameters.name: recordTypeId
parameters.description: The unique identifier of the record type to deprecate.

202.description: The deprecation request was accepted and the record type state transition has started.

400.description: The request is malformed.

403.description: The caller is authenticated but does not have permission to deprecate the record type.

404.description: No record type exists with the specified Id.

409.description: The record type already deprecated; or has an active draft.

422.description: The record type is actively attached to one or more published Media Profiles.

post.summary: Deprecate a field in a record type.
post.description: Document that deprecated fields remain readable on existing records but are excluded from validation on new record submissions. This is a softer alternative to removal — existing data is preserved and the field continues to appear in read models.

parameters.name: recordTypeId
parameters.description: The unique identifier of the record type containing the field to deprecate.

parameters.name: fieldName
parameters.description: The name of the field to deprecate.

202.description: The deprecation request was accepted and the record type update has started.

400.description: The request is malformed or contains invalid data.

403.description: The caller is authenticated but does not have permission to deprecate the field in the record type.

404.description: Field key not found on draft.

409.description: Field already deprecated; or no active draf.

post.summary: Add a field to a record type.
post.description: Document supported field types and validation rules. Fields are scoped to the draft and take effect on publish.

parameters.name: recordTypeId
parameters.description: The unique identifier of the record type to which the field will be added.

204.description: The field was successfully added to the record type.

400.description: Invalid field configuration — unrecognised type, missing required attributes.

403.description: The caller is authenticated but does not have permission to modify the record type.

404.description: No record type exists with the specified Id.

409.description: A field key already exists on this draft; or no active draft.

422.description: The specified data type for the field is invalid or unsupported.

post.summary: Add a capability to a record type.
post.description: Document that capabilities are added to the draft and take effect on publish. Document valid capability identifiers in the request body description.

parameters.name: recordTypeId
parameters.description: The unique identifier of the record type to which the capability will be added.

202.description: The capability was successfully added to the record type.

400.description: The request is malformed or contains invalid data.

403.description: The caller is authenticated but does not have permission to add a capability to the record type.

404.description: No record type exists with the specified Id.

409.description: The capability already added to this record type draft; or no active draft.

422.description: Unrecognised capability value.

post.description: Document the state transition: Draft → Submitted. An SQS event is published downstream to trigger external submission processing.

parameters.name: registrationId
parameters.description: The unique identifier of the registration to submit.

400.description: The request is invalid, for example required fields are missing or malformed.

403.description: The caller is authenticated but does not have permission to submit the registration.

404.description: The specified registration was not found.

409.description: The registration is not in Draft state — already submitted or in a terminal state.

422.description: No media items attached; or required registration metadata missing

post.description: Document the state transition: Rejected → Submitted. Owner should resolve rejection reasons before calling this endpoint.

parameters.name: registrationId
parameters.description: The unique identifier of the registration to resubmit.

400.description: The request is invalid, for example required fields are missing or malformed.

403.description: The caller is authenticated but does not have permission to resubmit the registration.

404.description: The specified registration was not found.

409.description: The registration is not in Rejected state — resubmission is only valid after a rejection.

422.description: the issues cited in the rejection have not been addressed — validation failures.

post.summary: Request an amendment to a registration.
post.description: Document that an amendment opens a new review cycle on an already-confirmed registration. Requires a reason for the amendment request.

parameters.name: registrationId
parameters.description: The unique identifier of the registration to amend.

400.description: The request is invalid, for example required fields are missing or malformed.

403.description: The caller is authenticated but does not have permission to request an amendment for this registration.

404.description: The specified registration was not found.

409.description: The amendment already in progress for this registration — only one active amendment permitted.

422.description: The registration is not in Confirmed state — amendments may only be raised against confirmed registrations.

post.description: Document the state transition: Submitted → Rejected. The rejection reason is stored on the event and exposed via the read model so the owner can correct and resubmit. Document the reason request body field.

parameters.name: registrationId
parameters.description: The unique identifier of the registration to reject.

400.description: The rejection reason required and must not be empty.

403.description: The caller is authenticated but does not have permission to reject the registration.

404.description: The specified registration was not found.

409.description: The registration is not in Submitted state.

422.description: The reason for rejection is missing or exceeds the maximum length.

post.summary: Reject an amendment to a registration.
post.description: Document that rejecting an amendment closes the amendment cycle and leaves the registration in its current Confirmed state — no changes are applied. The rejection reason is stored on the event and returned via the read model. The owner may raise a new amendment request after reviewing the rejection.

parameters.name: registrationId
parameters.description: The unique identifier of the registration with the amendment to reject.

parameters.name: amendmentId
parameters.description: The unique identifier of the amendment to reject.

400.description: The request is invalid, for example required fields are missing or malformed.

403.description: The caller is authenticated but does not have permission to reject the amendment.

404.description: The specified registration or amendment was not found.

409.description: No pending amendment on this registration.
